task_completion.description: Create the conditions.csv file with the required columns and 6 rows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or spreadsheet application showing a file named conditions.csv with columns: left_color, right_color, cue_dir, probe_color, corr_resp, and 6 rows of data.

task_completion.description: Create and save the PsychoPy experiment file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Builder window open with the title or save path indicating retrocue_task.psyexp.

task_completion.description: Add the required sequence of routines to the experiment flow.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flow panel at the bottom of the PsychoPy Builder shows the sequence of routines: encoding, delay1, cue, delay2, probe.

task_completion.description: Configure components (e.g., polygons, keyboard responses) within the routines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Routine panel in PsychoPy Builder shows components such as polygons (for squares) or keyboard responses added to the routines.

task_completion.description: Add a loop around the routines to connect the conditions file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flow panel in PsychoPy Builder shows a loop encompassing the routines, indicating it is set up to iterate over the conditions.

task_completion.description: Run the experiment to generate data in the data/ folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the experiment actively running (e.g., a window displaying colored squares), the PsychoPy Runner window indicating execution, or a file manager/terminal showing files in the data/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the PsychoPy Builder interface (adding routines, components, loops) rather than just writing a .psyexp XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the experiment was actually executed to generate data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of the PsychoPy Runner window or the actual experiment display window, rather than just using terminal commands to create fake data files.

integrity.description: Verify the data files were generated by PsychoPy and not manually created.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data directory should contain authentic PsychoPy output files (.csv, .psydat, .log) generated after a run, not empty files created via 'touch'.

Data must be generated in /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/retrocue/data.
